source.input_excerpt: A clinic intake coordinator is reviewing administrative readiness for a new appointment request. The request says to build from the intake_packet data model, eligibility API status, document fields, JSON schema, prompt template, tool call r

activity_model.activity: Deciding whether the packet is ready to schedule, should be returned for missing consent, or needs insurance verification escalation
activity_model.objective: Whether the packet is ready to schedule, should be returned for missing consent, or needs insurance verification escalation
outcomes: An intake handoff with the next administrative action and reason
domain_vocabulary: administrative readiness for a new appointment request

interaction_contract.primary_decision: Whether the packet is ready to schedule, should be returned for missing consent, or needs insurance verification escalation
next_actions: The outcome is an intake handoff with the next administrative action and reason
Use domain terms: intake packet, referral, insurance verification, signed consent, appointment slot, administrative handoff
interaction_contract.completion: An intake handoff with the next administrative action and reason
make_easy: Confirm the activity model before screen structure
Review the primary decision and outcome in domain language
Adjust vocabulary before implementation detail reaches the product UI

work_units: Review referral, insurance, and required forms
Choose schedule, return for forms, or insurance verification

stepper_eligibility.reason: No staged-flow intent detected; use topology, work units, and coordinated surfaces instead.

decision_points: Decide whether the packet is administratively ready, missing required consent, or needs insurance verification
workflow.completion_state: The intake team receives the next administrative action and reason

surface_set.name: Administrative intake workspace
surface_set.purpose: Choose an intake packet, review readiness evidence, decide the administrative path, and send a handoff
surface_set.relationship_to_workflow: Keeps intake readiness evidence, administrative decision controls, and handoff state coordinated

product_terms: administrative readiness for a new appointment request

handoff.reason: Signed consent is missing before the appointment can be scheduled
handoff.next_action: Return the packet for consent and keep the request in administrative review

disclosure_reminders.product_ui_rule: Keep implementation details and JudgmentKit review-packet terms out of product UI.

evidence: complete desktop and mobile browser QA

implementation_contract.purpose: Give agents an enforceable implementation gate after activity/workflow judgment, so generated UI uses approved project primitives instead of ad hoc controls
authority_order: repo-local interface contract and helper primitives
external product UI system evidence when explicitly provided

required_evidence: controls preserve target size and spacing
validation and disabled reasons are readable

default_rules: feature render paths must not emit raw input, select, textarea, or checkbox markup outside approved helpers
new primitives or variants require updating the repo-local contract first
generated UI must include a local check command or equivalent static inspection

checks: keyboard focus check for interactive controls
overflow check for long labels, descriptions, and values
modal or dialog footer order check when dialogs are present
disabled, empty, error, and ready state review

default_ai_native_design_system.purpose: Govern agent-generated UI at the contract layer before any adapter-layer renderer choices
primitive_defaults: use only approved implementation primitives or documented repo-local equivalents
new primitives require contract evidence before use
raw controls stay behind approved helper primitives
surface_patterns: workbench surfaces keep selection, evidence, decision, and completion state adjacent
operator review surfaces keep produced work, evidence, risk, bounded decision, and receipt together
form flows keep labels, validation, review, submit, and confirmation in task order
setup and debug surfaces may expose implementation detail only when that is the activity
state_rules: show disabled, error, loading, empty, ready, and focus-visible states with readable reasons
state names must describe user-visible work state instead of internal lifecycle mechanics

required: primary actions must come from the workflow, domain decision, or explicit handoff path
committing, destructive, financial, publishing, or external side-effect actions require an explicit approval boundary
diagnostic or setup actions stay secondary unless the activity is setup, debugging, auditing, or integration
completion actions leave a receipt, result, or handoff reason
failure_signals: a risky action is exposed without explicit user approval or confirmation evidence
an unauthorized action is listed in the generated UI candidate
a diagnostic action is promoted as a primary work action outside setup, debugging, auditing, or integration
completion can occur without a receipt, result, or handoff reason
product UI text exposes diagnostic-only terms outside an allowed diagnostic context
source identifiers, prompts, schemas, traces, or tool mechanics appear as user-facing work vocabulary
data fields are listed because they exist rather than because they support the activity

evidence_gates: action boundary evidence when risky actions are present
data visibility evidence when primary text or fields are supplied
desktop and mobile browser QA evidence

adapter_boundary.visual_token_adapter: boundary-only metadata lives at implementation_contract.visual_token_adapter; renderer use remains deferred
adapter_boundary.renderer_package: deferred until a token adapter can drive rendered primitives without changing the activity contract

component_contracts.label: Action button
use_when: the user can commit, cancel, navigate, disclose, or retry a clear action
avoid_when: the action is a passive label, status, or unsupported shortcut
failure_signals: icon-only button has no accessible name
destructive action appears without confirmation evidence

component_contracts.label: Action group
component_contracts.purpose: Group related actions with clear priority and boundaries
use_when: several actions affect the same object or decision
avoid_when: unrelated actions compete for primary emphasis
review_checks: secondary actions remain adjacent to their object
failure_signals: multiple primary actions compete
actions are detached from the evidence they affect

component_contracts.label: Form field
component_contracts.purpose: Collect one labeled value with help, error, and disabled affordances
use_when: the user enters or changes structured information
avoid_when: the value is read-only evidence or status
failure_signals: placeholder is the only label
error is color-only

component_contracts.label: Text field
component_contracts.purpose: Collect short text or numeric values
use_when: the input is short, single-line, and directly editable
avoid_when: long-form text, selection, or multi-value choice is required
failure_signals: raw input replaces approved primitive
invalid state lacks text feedback

component_contracts.label: Text area
use_when: the user writes notes, reasons, instructions, or descriptions
avoid_when: a short value or fixed selection is enough
review_checks: long content does not collapse nearby controls
failure_signals: text area is used for structured choices
long text overlaps following content

component_contracts.label: Select field
component_contracts.purpose: Choose one value from a bounded option set
use_when: options are known and one option is selected
avoid_when: free text or multiple independent choices are needed
review_checks: empty option is clear when optional
failure_signals: custom select lacks keyboard evidence
unassigned state is ambiguous

component_contracts.label: Checkbox group
component_contracts.purpose: Choose independent options from a set
use_when: multiple options can be selected independently
avoid_when: the user must choose exactly one option
failure_signals: checkboxes behave like radios
group has no legend

component_contracts.label: Radio group
component_contracts.purpose: Choose exactly one option from a set
failure_signals: mutually exclusive options are shown as checkboxes
selected state is visual-only

component_contracts.label: Toggle
component_contracts.purpose: Switch a binary setting on or off
use_when: the setting changes between two persistent states
avoid_when: the action is one-time, destructive, or requires explanation before commit
review_checks: state change is reversible or bounded
failure_signals: toggle hides the current state
toggle performs a destructive action

component_contracts.label: Tabs
component_contracts.purpose: Switch between peer sections without changing the task object
use_when: sections are peers and the user compares or switches views
avoid_when: the flow is ordered or requires completion before the next step
failure_signals: tabs are used as a wizard
inactive panels are unreachable by keyboard

component_contracts.label: Menu
component_contracts.purpose: Reveal a compact set of contextual commands
use_when: secondary commands need disclosure near their object
avoid_when: primary decisions or required information are hidden
failure_signals: required action is hidden in a menu
menu traps focus

component_contracts.label: Dialog
component_contracts.purpose: Interrupt the current surface for a bounded decision or focused form
use_when: the user must confirm, complete, or inspect a focused task before returning
avoid_when: content can stay inline or the decision is not blocking
review_checks: cancel or dismiss precedes primary action
failure_signals: dialog lacks dismiss path
focus is not returned after close

component_contracts.label: Alert
component_contracts.purpose: Call attention to important status, risk, or required action
use_when: the user needs immediate status, warning, error, or success context
avoid_when: the message is ordinary helper text
failure_signals: color is the only severity cue
alert repeats unrelated information

component_contracts.label: Table
use_when: the user scans, compares, sorts, or verifies tabular records
avoid_when: cards or prose better support the activity
review_checks: empty and loading states are clear
failure_signals: fields are shown only because they exist
wide table overflows without review

component_contracts.label: Panel
component_contracts.purpose: Group related work, evidence, or controls in a bounded region
use_when: a region needs a readable boundary and heading
avoid_when: nested cards or decorative wrappers add noise
failure_signals: card inside card nesting creates visual clutter
panel title is generic

component_contracts.label: Card
component_contracts.purpose: Summarize one repeated item or choice
use_when: items repeat and each needs a compact summary
avoid_when: the layout becomes a decorative page section
failure_signals: page sections are styled as cards
clickable card lacks keyboard evidence

component_contracts.label: Status message
component_contracts.purpose: Report progress, result, validation, or completion
use_when: the user needs to know what happened or what to do next
avoid_when: status is decorative or duplicates nearby text
failure_signals: spinner has no status text
success state lacks receipt or next action

pattern_contracts.label: Marketing surface
pattern_contracts.purpose: Orient a visitor to an offer and move them to a clear next step
pattern_contracts.completion_or_handoff: visitor understands the offer and can take the next step
pattern_contracts.disclosure_boundary: implementation detail stays out of public persuasion copy
failure_signals: decorative polish replaces proof
primary offer is unclear

pattern_contracts.label: Workbench
pattern_contracts.purpose: Help a user repeatedly inspect, compare, decide, and act
pattern_contracts.completion_or_handoff: selected work item advances or leaves a clear handoff
pattern_contracts.disclosure_boundary: diagnostics stay secondary unless the activity is inspection
failure_signals: queue rows explain too much while detail lacks evidence
decision controls are detached from evidence

pattern_contracts.label: Operator review
pattern_contracts.purpose: Review produced work, evidence, risk, and a bounded next action
pattern_contracts.completion_or_handoff: review produces a decision reason and receipt
pattern_contracts.disclosure_boundary: raw system mechanics appear only as diagnostic evidence
failure_signals: system output is trusted without evidence
risky action has no approval boundary

pattern_contracts.label: Form flow
pattern_contracts.purpose: Collect or change structured information with validation
pattern_contracts.completion_or_handoff: valid information is saved or submitted with confirmation
pattern_contracts.disclosure_boundary: field labels use domain language instead of schema names
failure_signals: validation is color-only
schema fields are exposed as product labels

pattern_contracts.label: Dashboard monitor
pattern_contracts.purpose: Track status, exceptions, trends, or operational health
pattern_contracts.completion_or_handoff: user knows current state and whether follow-up is needed
pattern_contracts.disclosure_boundary: metrics explain operational meaning, not storage or API mechanics
failure_signals: metrics lack decision meaning
charts carry meaning without text fallback

pattern_contracts.label: Content or report
pattern_contracts.purpose: Help the user read, understand, cite, or share information
pattern_contracts.completion_or_handoff: reader understands, cites, exports, or shares the material
pattern_contracts.disclosure_boundary: source mechanics appear only when provenance is part of the report
failure_signals: report is broken into app chrome without reading hierarchy
references are missing

pattern_contracts.label: Setup or debugging tool
pattern_contracts.purpose: Configure, inspect, test, or troubleshoot machinery
pattern_contracts.completion_or_handoff: setup is valid or failure has a cause and next fix
pattern_contracts.disclosure_boundary: implementation detail is allowed because it is the work material
failure_signals: diagnostics appear without remediation
raw logs replace user-facing result

pattern_contracts.label: Conversation
pattern_contracts.purpose: Support open-ended exchange where the thread is the product surface
pattern_contracts.completion_or_handoff: conversation continues, recovers, or closes with context intact
pattern_contracts.disclosure_boundary: system traces stay hidden unless explicitly requested
failure_signals: message state is visual-only
composer lacks recovery after failure

iteration_policy.judgmentkit_role: review generated UI evidence and return structured failures; do not mutate files or call a provider model

design_system_source.name: JudgmentKit

icon_catalog.notice: Some Lucide icons are derived from Feather Icons and retain the Feather MIT notice; see THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md
icon_catalog.style_system: Lucide 24px outline icons with round caps and joins

provenance_rules: visual tokens, fonts, icons, and renderer components must come from this active design-system source
local CSS may define layout and UI structure but must not become the source of visual tokens, typography, icon assets, or renderer components
external design systems must be supplied as complete contract-time adapters; missing authorities do not fall back to JudgmentKit defaults

visual_token_adapter.purpose: Define semantic token, font, and icon evidence after contract governance without selecting renderer components

token_roles.usage: backgrounds, panels, overlays, and work-surface regions
body copy, headings, labels, and dense operational text
control boundaries, dividers, panels, and grouped evidence
visible focus indicators and keyboard navigation affordances
success, warning, error, loading, and empty states
primary decision controls and bounded action groups
risk, escalation, destructive, or externally committing actions
disabled controls and unavailable state reasons
completion, confirmation, and handoff receipt states

css_custom_properties.name: --jk-color-surface
css_custom_properties.usage: panels, cards, overlays, and work regions

css_custom_properties.name: --jk-color-border
css_custom_properties.usage: dividers, control outlines, and grouped evidence

css_custom_properties.name: --jk-color-focus
css_custom_properties.usage: visible focus rings and active affordances

css_custom_properties.name: --jk-color-risk
css_custom_properties.usage: risk, escalation, and destructive action states

css_custom_properties.name: --jk-space-3
css_custom_properties.usage: row gaps and adjacent evidence spacing

css_custom_properties.name: --jk-radius-panel
css_custom_properties.usage: cards, panels, and bounded work areas

font_roles.usage: primary product text and work-surface copy
form labels, metadata labels, tabs, and compact control text
counts, prices, times, IDs, and values that need stable width

font_roles.stack: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, "Liberation Mono", monospace
font_roles.usage: diagnostic-only identifiers, logs, code, and implementation details in allowed contexts

font_rules: judgmentkit_design_system uses system font stacks and does not load remote font files
diagnostic font roles may appear only in setup, debugging, auditing, integration, or explicit source-inspection contexts
numeric font roles should preserve readable alignment with tabular numbers when supported

icon_catalog.notice: Some Lucide icons are derived from Feather Icons and retain the Feather MIT notice; see THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md
icon_catalog.style_system: Lucide 24px outline icons with round caps and joins

selection_flow: Use search_icon_catalog to find candidate Lucide icons by activity meaning, source icon name, alias, or tag
Use get_icon_svg for the selected canonical Lucide icon id before rendering inline SVG
Use list_icon_catalog for pagination or category browsing; existing context tools intentionally return only this catalog summary
icon_selection_policy.semantic_guidance: JudgmentKit may recommend roles such as status icon, handoff icon, or risk icon; agents choose concrete Lucide icon ids through the catalog tools
accessibility_guidance: Icon use never satisfies accessibility evidence by itself
Icon-only controls require accessible names plus target-size and keyboard/focus evidence
Meaningful icons require adjacent visible text when possible and non-text contrast evidence
failure_signals: selected icon ids are not canonical ids in the Lucide catalog
icons are used as a substitute for required labels, states, activity evidence, workflow evidence, accessibility evidence, or QA gates
icons are fetched from a runtime CDN or remote source instead of the committed MCP catalog

icon_rules: default icons come from the committed Lucide catalog exposed through MCP icon tools
normal implementation context includes catalog summary and policy only, not the full catalog payload
icons that convey meaning require adjacent text or an accessible name
icon-only controls require target-size and keyboard/focus evidence
meaningful icons require non-text contrast evidence
adapter_rules: visual token, font, and icon evidence describes semantic roles and constraints; it does not create approved primitives
visual token, font, and icon evidence cannot satisfy missing activity, primitive, state, action-boundary, data-visibility, accessibility, static-check, or browser-QA gates
token, font, and icon names stay adapter metadata and must not become product UI vocabulary
renderer and component packages remain deferred until the token adapter can drive rendered primitives without changing the activity contract
evidence_expectations: name visual token families used by the candidate
map token semantics to approved primitives, states, and surface patterns
name font roles and confirm system stacks or repo-approved font assets
name icon roles and selected canonical Lucide icon ids or repo-approved icon assets
include accessibility-relevant token evidence when color, motion, density, focus, or status roles affect readability or input
failure_signals: visual token, font, or icon evidence is used as a substitute for approved primitives or required states
visual token, font, or icon evidence claims to pass accessibility, action, data-visibility, static, or browser gates without the required evidence
unsupported token families, font roles, or icon roles are introduced without adapter evidence
renderer, component package, catalog, compiler, or A2UI work is introduced in the boundary-only slice

appearance_policy.mode_resolution: system follows the user's operating-system or browser color-scheme preference
appearance_policy.visible_toggle_policy: Do not show an appearance toggle by default; add one only when the product activity requires a persistent user preference
appearance_policy.persistence_policy: Do not store an appearance preference by default; system detection is the portable default

css_custom_properties.name: --jk-color-surface
css_custom_properties.usage: panels, cards, overlays, and work regions

css_custom_properties.name: --jk-color-border
css_custom_properties.usage: dividers, control outlines, and grouped evidence

css_custom_properties.name: --jk-color-focus
css_custom_properties.usage: visible focus rings and active affordances

css_custom_properties.name: --jk-color-risk
css_custom_properties.usage: risk, escalation, and destructive action states

css_custom_properties.name: --jk-space-3
css_custom_properties.usage: row gaps and adjacent evidence spacing

css_custom_properties.name: --jk-radius-panel
css_custom_properties.usage: cards, panels, and bounded work areas

css_custom_properties.name: --jk-color-surface
css_custom_properties.usage: panels, cards, overlays, and work regions

css_custom_properties.name: --jk-color-border
css_custom_properties.usage: dividers, control outlines, and grouped evidence

css_custom_properties.name: --jk-color-focus
css_custom_properties.usage: visible focus rings and active affordances

css_custom_properties.name: --jk-color-risk
css_custom_properties.usage: risk, escalation, and destructive action states

css_custom_properties.name: --jk-space-3
css_custom_properties.usage: row gaps and adjacent evidence spacing

css_custom_properties.name: --jk-radius-panel
css_custom_properties.usage: cards, panels, and bounded work areas

applies_when: the UI or design spec calls for substantive visuals, imagery, media, domain scenes, product/place/object visuals, or spatial/data visualization where visual quality is part of the expected result
preferred_paths: use imagegen for substantive bitmap imagery, scenes, product/place/object visuals, and media assets
use premium Three.js or WebGL rendering when the visual is an interactive or spatial 3D experience
use D3 or an equivalent high-quality visualization renderer when the visual is data-driven and clarity depends on the visualization
deterministic_safe_uses: UI chrome, icons, controls, and state indicators
simple diagrams or charts where clarity is the goal
accessible fallback structure and alt text
failure_signals: rudimentary CSS, SVG, canvas, or JavaScript geometry is used as a final substitute for substantive imagery
decorative procedural blocks stand in for a real product, place, object, domain scene, or media asset
image generation or premium rendering is skipped without rationale when the spec calls for substantive visuals

rendered_background_readability.requirement: Text over rendered visual backgrounds must provide browser-rendered contrast/readability evidence, not screenshots alone

applies_when: text appears over images, canvas, WebGL, video, gradients, generated visuals, or other rendered backgrounds
icons, charts, state indicators, custom control boundaries, or graphical objects convey meaning
custom colors, gradients, overlays, or authored focus indicators are used
dense controls, icon-only controls, touch targets, toolbar actions, or pointer targets are present
sticky layers, overlays, dialogs, modals, popovers, or persistent disclosed panels can cover focusable content
custom widgets, dialogs, modals, canvas/WebGL interactions, rich editors, or embedded experiences can receive focus
animation, reveal transitions, parallax, WebGL motion, canvas motion, or generated visual motion is present
moving, blinking, scrolling, auto-updating, or auto-advancing content starts without direct user control
tooltips, popovers, hover cards, or focus-triggered content appears
forms, data-entry controls, required fields, or validation constraints are present
forms can reject input, show validation errors, or require correction
success, error, loading, progress, results, or async updates are shown without moving focus
audio, video, generated media, animated media, or time-based media appears
canvas, WebGL, Three.js, D3, generated imagery, charts, or custom-rendered visuals convey content or controls

requirements: normal text meets 4.5:1 and large text meets 3:1 contrast targets
meaningful non-text visual information meets 3:1 contrast against adjacent colors
text over rendered visual backgrounds uses browser-rendered or computed evidence
all functionality has a keyboard path
focus order preserves meaning and operation
custom widgets honor expected WAI-ARIA APG keyboard behavior
content structure uses semantic HTML or equivalent accessible structure
interactive controls expose accessible names, roles, states, and values
inputs provide visible labels or instructions
detected input errors identify the field and describe the problem in text
status messages are programmatically determinable without requiring focus
non-essential interaction-triggered animation can be disabled or follows reduced-motion preferences
auto-starting motion, blinking, scrolling, or updating content can be paused, stopped, hidden, or controlled
media includes captions, transcripts, audio descriptions, or equivalent alternatives when applicable
content reflows without two-dimensional scrolling at narrow widths unless two-dimensional layout is essential
pointer targets meet minimum target size or valid spacing/equivalent-control exceptions

failure_signals: text over images, canvas, WebGL, video, gradients, or generated visuals is illegible
opacity-based reveal transitions pass through low-contrast states
browser-rendered contrast/readability evidence is skipped for text over visual backgrounds
automated accessibility scans are provided without browser-rendered or manual evidence for keyboard, focus, semantics, responsive behavior, or rendered backgrounds
forms or status messages rely on visual-only error or success cues
custom widgets expose roles without matching keyboard behavior, focus management, or state/value semantics
raw form controls appear in feature render code
checkboxes or labels rely on browser defaults without approved spacing
a new variant is invented without contract evidence
non-destructive modal or dialog actions put cancel/dismiss after the primary completion action
focus, disabled, or error states are missing
browser QA is skipped after UI generation
